perturbation.original_text: 2.2 NeuroBo shall manufacture, or have manufactured, and supply to Dong-A the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), which are necessary to manufacture the Licensed Products, in the quantity and in conformity with the specifications separately agreed upon between the Parties and attached hereto as Exhibit C (the "API Specifications") as may be amended by the Parties's agreement in writing from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 NeuroBo shall manufacture, or have manufactured, and supply to Dong-A the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), which are necessary to manufacture the Licensed Products. The specifications will be determined later.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of "in the quantity and in conformity with the specifications separately agreed upon between the Parties and attached hereto as Exhibit C (the "API Specifications") as may be amended by the Parties's agreement in writing from time to time" removes the requirement that API supplied by NeuroBo must meet agreed-upon specifications. This creates a contradiction because Section 2.4 later references the 'API Specifications' and the 'API Test'. By omitting the reference to Exhibit C, Section 2.2 contradicts Section 2.4, because the API specifications are not well defined, which creates ambiguity about quality control. This weakens the contract because it is hard to enforce specification and quality control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Within [***] days after receipt of the API from NeuroBo, Dong-A shall perform quality control test (the "API Test") in accordance with the methods of the API Test on such API for acceptance (the "API Test Methods"), which shall be separately agreed in writing by and between the Parties and attached hereto as Exhibit D") as may be amended by the Parties's agreement in writing from time to time. NeuroBo shall provide Dong-A with all available information and technical assistance necessary for Dong-A to perform the API Test expeditiously. If the API Test indicates that the API is deficient in quantity or does not meet the API Specifications, Dong-A shall notify NeuroBo thereof in writing within the [***]-day period together with results of the API Test. If the quantity is deficient, NeuroBo shall, as soon as commercially reasonable, ship, or have shipped, the sufficient amount of additional API to cover the deficiency. If the API does not meet the API Specifications, NeuroBo shall retrieve the API at its own expense and replace the API at no additional cost to Dong-A.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.2 omits the requirement that the API supplied by NeuroBo must conform to agreed-upon specifications (Exhibit C). This directly contradicts Section 2.4, which relies on the existence of these 'API Specifications' for quality control testing ('API Test'). Without defined specifications, the 'API Test' in Section 2.4 becomes meaningless, creating a significant inconsistency and making it unclear how API quality will be determined. The changed text makes the contract unenforceable regarding specifications and quality control.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Dong-A shall supply NeuroBo with the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo together with a certificate of analysis, as described in the Product Specifications, for each batch of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo shipped hereunder certifying that such batch of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo meets the Product Specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Dong-A shall supply NeuroBo with the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo together with a certificate of analysis for each batch of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo shipped hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase ", as described in the Product Specifications, for each batch of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo shipped hereunder certifying that such batch of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo meets the Product Specifications.", Section 3.2 no longer explicitly requires the certificate of analysis to confirm compliance with the Product Specifications. However, Section 3.3 depends on the products meeting the product specifications for product acceptance. The omission creates an in-text contradiction, as the explicit requirement for the certificate of analysis to verify compliance is removed, creating ambiguity and weakens the contract's enforceability regarding product quality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Within [***] days after receipt of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo hereunder, NeuroBo may, in its discretion, perform a quality control test (the "Product Test") in accordance with the methods of the test on such Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo for acceptance (the "Product Test Methods"), which shall be separately agreed in writing by and between Dong-A and NeuroBo and attached hereto as Exhibit B, as may be amended by the Parties' agreement in writing from time to time. Dong-A shall provide NeuroBo with all available information and technical assistance necessary for NeuroBo to perform the Product Test expeditiously. If the Product Test indicates that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo is deficient in quantity or does not meet the Product Specifications, NeuroBo shall notify Dong-A thereof in writing within the [***]-day period together with results of the Product Test. If the quantity is deficient, Dong-A shall immediately ship the sufficient amount of additional Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo to cover the deficiency. If Dong-A does not agree that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, the Parties shall refer their disagreement for decision by an independent testing laboratory agreed by the Parties. The decision by the independent testing laboratory shall be conclusive and binding on both Parties, and the losing Party shall bear the costs of the independent testing laboratory. If Dong-A agrees that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, or if the decision by the testing laboratory confirms that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, (i) Dong-A shall arrange for the return from NeuroBo of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at Dong-A's expense, and (ii) without waiting for the return, Dong-A shall promptly replace the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at no additional cost to NeuroBo.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3.2 explicitly required the certificate of analysis to confirm compliance with the Product Specifications. The changed text removes this requirement, creating ambiguity as to whether the certificate of analysis must confirm compliance. Section 3.3 relies on the Licensed Products meeting the Product Specifications for product acceptance and testing. Because the explicit requirement for verification is removed in 3.2, it contradicts 3.3 and weakens the contract's enforceability regarding product quality, fitting the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 The supply prices for the Licensed Products and their matching placebo shall be specified in Schedule 4.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 The supply prices for the Licensed Products and their matching placebo will be determined.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall be specified in Schedule 4.2" to "will be determined", the contract lacks a defined pricing mechanism, contradicting the requirements set forth in Section 4.1: "The terms and conditions of sale and purchase of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo between Dong-A and NeuroBo shall be set forth in each Firm Order." If there is no specified supply price, than there is an incomplete condition for sale.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 The terms and conditions of sale and purchase of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo between Dong-A and NeuroBo shall be set forth in each Firm Order.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be specified in Schedule 4.2' to 'will be determined' removes a defined pricing mechanism, which is essential for the 'terms and conditions of sale and purchase' as stated in Section 4.1. This omission creates a direct contradiction because Section 4.1 requires the terms to be set forth in each Firm Order, but the changed text leaves the pricing undetermined, making it impossible to fulfill the requirement of Section 4.1. Therefore, a firm order can not be created based on incomplete information.
